title: 10 best herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_properties: Emollient property: Herbal aloe creams moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing dryness and discomfort. This property helps to lock in moisture and reduce flakiness.

Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting a faster recovery from lip irritation.

Antioxidant property: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to dryness and lip lines, helping to prevent premature aging of the lips.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with dry lips because of its natural moisturizing and soothing properties. The gel inside the aloe plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which work together to hydrate and calm dry, chapped lips. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and irritation, leaving lips soft, smooth, and protected from further dryness. Regular use of aloe vera creams can provide long-lasting relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Aliphatic Acids: These compounds, particularly glycerol and mucopolysaccharides, help to retain moisture in the skin, providing relief from dryness and irritation on the lips.

Polysaccharides (Mucopolysaccharides): They act as a natural humectant, drawing in moisture from the air and locking it into the skin, thus preventing dryness and promoting the natural healing process of the lips.

Vitamins and Minerals (especially Vitamin E and Zinc): These essential nutrients help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of healthy cells, which can help to alleviate dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Aloe vera gel, which is rich in soothing and moisturizing properties that help to heal and protect dry lips.

Gel (from Leaves): It's the most commonly used part of Aloe vera as it is easily extracted and has a high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to hydrate and soften dry lips.

Juice (from Leaves): The juice from the leaves of Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce lip inflammation and promote healing, making it an effective ingredient in creams for dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 large aloe vera leaves from mature plants and cut them into small pieces to extract gel.
Weigh out 200g of aloe vera gel and mix with 50g of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil to the mixture and heat it gently over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool until the mixture reaches 40°c then whisk in 5g of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry, chapped lips, promoting healthy lip skin.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips, providing soothing relief and comfort.
Emollient Properties: Herbal extracts like aloe vera and chamomile in the cream help lock in moisture, hydrate, and soften dry, chapped lip skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with dry lips because of its exceptional moisturizing and soothing properties. The active ingredient, melaleuca oil, contains antifungal and antibacterial compounds that combat infections and inflammation, allowing the skin to heal. Additionally, tea tree creams provide long-lasting hydration, helping to repair and protect dry, chapped lips. The natural ingredients work in harmony to provide a barrier against environmental stressors, leaving lips smooth, soft, and refreshed. This makes it an excellent solution for dry lip relief.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These phenolic compounds help to soothe and protect dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thus alleviating discomfort and promoting healthy skin recovery.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to moisturize and protect dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting a natural barrier function to prevent further moisture loss and dryness.

Methyl eugenol: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry, chapped lips, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent infections and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia contain high amounts of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial and moisturizing properties that help soothe and protect dry lips.
Seeds: The seeds contain oil that is rich in antioxidants and moisturizing compounds, which help to hydrate and soften dry lips.
Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of tea tree oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted supplier to use in your recipe.
Grind the dried leaves using a coffee grinder into a fine powder within 30 minutes.
Combine 100g of the powdered leaves with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Mix 50g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter with the beeswax and powdered leaves mixture.
Pour the mixture into a tin container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips, providing instant relief from irritation.
Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate dry, cracked lips, promoting a healthy environment for lip healing.

Soothing and calming property: The apigenin and luteolin present in chamomile have a sedative effect on the skin, helping to calm and soothe dry, irritated lips, reducing discomfort and promoting moisturization.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which help to calm and protect dry, chapped lips. The cream's hydrating properties also help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief and comfort to dry, irritated lips. This natural remedy provides a gentle, non-irritating solution for dry lip care.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and moisturize dry lips.
Chamazulene: This azulene derivative has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect and soothe dry, irritated skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and moisturize dry lips.
Seeds: They are used for their emollient properties, providing hydration and nourishment to dry lips.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant properties, helping to protect and soothe dry and chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then let it cool to room temperature in 30 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the cooled chamomilla infusion with 2 ounces of distilled water and 1 ounce of beeswax.
Stir the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens in 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken to a creamy consistency in 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation and dryness on the skin, soothing and healing cracked lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and hydrated appearance.
Moisturizing properties: Lavender's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked lips by reducing water loss and creating a protective barrier on the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to calm and comfort dry, chapped lips. Additionally, the rich emollients in lavender creams help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting hydration and relief from dryness and discomfort. This makes lavender creams a popular natural remedy for dry, chapped lips, providing a gentle and effective solution for maintaining healthy, smooth lips.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to soothe and moisturize dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to protect dry lips from infection and promote healthy skin regeneration.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and promote the repair of dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used due to their calming and soothing properties, which help to moisturize and protect dry lips.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Lavender seeds are sometimes used in skincare products due to their high content of essential fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and nourish dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a warm sunny day in mid-summer.
Steep the harvested flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth to remove the flowers and obtain a liquid extract.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused liquid extract with the beeswax mixture and whip until smooth and creamy texture forms.

remedy_properties: Emollient and Humectant Properties: Marshmallow creams contain emollients like glycosides, which fill in the gaps between skin cells to lock in moisture and soften dry, chapped lips. This helps to hydrate and soothe dryness.

Anti-Inflammatory and Anti-Irritant Properties: The saponins present in marshmallows have anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, while also calming irritation and inflammation.

Mucilages and Gums: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages like galactomannans, which form a protective film on the skin's surface, providing a barrier against environmental stressors and preventing moisture loss, further helping to soothe and hydrate dry lips.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a protective barrier on the skin's surface. This barrier helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and discomfort. As a result, Althaea officinalis creams can provide long-lasting relief for dry, chapped lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. It's a natural solution for dry lip care.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing irritation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and redness associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting healing and comfort.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, including althaea triterpenoids, have emollient and moisturizing properties that help to hydrate and soften dry, flaky skin on the lips, leaving them smooth and supple.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain mucilage, a natural emollient that helps to moisturize and soothe dry lips.
Leaves: They are a rich source of glycoproteins, which help to lock in moisture and protect dry lips from further irritation.
Stems: They also contain mucilage, which, like the roots, helps to hydrate and calm dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried roots of althaea officinalis from a trusted supplier and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a saucepan and heat gently.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 hours to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Mix the strained oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of cocoa butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Calendula Oil's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Calendula oil, extracted from pot marigold flowers, has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry lips.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Activity: The antioxidants and antimicrobial agents present in pot marigold creams help protect the lips from environmental stressors and prevent infections, promoting healthy lip tissue and preventing further dryness.

Emollient and Moisturizing Properties: Pot marigold creams contain emollients that lock in moisture, softening and hydrating dry, chapped lips, while also helping to repair and restore the skin's natural barrier function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with dry lips because of its rich anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and saponins, help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. By locking in moisture and protecting the skin from environmental stressors, Calendula creams create an ideal environment for lip tissue repair, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and healthy-looking. Regular use can also help prevent dry lip flakiness and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help with dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, ultimately soothing and calming dry, chapped skin.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Calendula creams, which can help to repair and protect dry, cracked lips from further damage.

Naphthoquinones (Plumbagin): Plumbagin, a type of naphthoquinone, exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity, which can help to reduce redness, soothe irritation, and promote the healing of dry, irritated lip tissues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm and moisturize dry lips.
Leaves: They are used because of their emollient and protective properties, which help to lock in moisture and shield dry lips from the environment.
Seeds: They are used because of their oil-rich content, which provides hydration and nourishment to dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks to remove excess moisture.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers in 8 ounces of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the calendula infused oil with a moisturizing base such as aloe vera gel or beeswax to create a soothing cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Geranium oil contains antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as UV radiation and pollution, which can contribute to dryness and chapping.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in geranium oil, such as citronellol and geraniol, help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting a more comfortable and hydrated appearance.

Moisturizing Properties: Geranium cream's moisturizing properties, which come from the fatty acid content of the herb, help lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, leaving them soft and supple.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pelargonium graveolens, also known as geranium, creams helps with dry lips because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm dry, chapped skin. The cream's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and cracks on the lips. Additionally, its antifungal properties prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate dryness, allowing the skin to repair itself naturally. Regular use can lead to smoother, softer, and healthier-looking lips.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps protect the lips from environmental stressors, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of dry, chapped skin.
Geraniol: As a terpene, geraniol possesses antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections and promote the healing of dry, cracked lips.
Citronellol: This terpene also exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect dry lips from further irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used due to their high content of geraniol, a compound that provides moisturizing and soothing properties.
Flowers: They are commonly used because of their ability to provide a pleasant fragrance and moisturizing properties, which help to soften and hydrate dry lips.
Roots: They are sometimes used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested materials in a warm place at 35c for 2 weeks to preserve the essential oils.
Steep 50g of dried pelargonium graveolens in 500ml of jojoba oil at 40c for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 50g of the infused oil and 5g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Cacao cream's antioxidants help to protect the lip skin from environmental stressors and damage, promoting healthy cell growth and reducing inflammation, which can exacerbate dryness.

Emollient and Moisturizing Properties: The fatty acids and glycerides in cacao cream create a barrier on the lip surface, locking in moisture and protecting against water loss, soothing and hydrating dry, chapped lips.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Cacao's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips, promoting comfort and relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Theobroma cacao, also known as cacao, creams helps with dry lips because of its rich moisturizing properties and antioxidants. The cacao plant contains essential fatty acids and vitamins A and E, which work together to lock in moisture and protect the lips from environmental stressors. Theobroma cacao also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm dry, irritated lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and hydrated. This natural ingredient is perfect for those seeking a gentle and effective solution to dry, chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help protect and soothe dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production, resulting in smoother and more hydrated skin.

Phenolic acids (Epicatechin and Epigallocatechin): As powerful antioxidants, these compounds help combat free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry, chapped lips, and promote a healthy, balanced skin environment.
Theobromine: This alkaloid has natural moisturizing and emollient properties, helping to lock in moisture and protect the skin from dryness and irritation, resulting in softer, more supple lips.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Theobroma cacao are primarily used to make creams for dry lips due to their rich content of theobromine and fatty acids, which provide moisturizing and soothing properties.

Fruits: The fruits of Theobroma cacao, also known as cacao pods, are used to make creams for dry lips due to their high content of antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to nourish and protect dry skin.

Seeds (again, as part of cacao butter): The seeds of Theobroma cacao are also used to make cacao butter, which is a key ingredient in many lip balms and creams for dry lips, providing moisturizing and protective properties.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of theobroma cacao nibs in a double boiler at 105°f to 115°f for 30 minutes.
Whisk in 1/2 cup of coconut milk and 1 tablespoon of maple syrup for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract and 1/2 teaspoon of salt to the mixture and whisk for 2 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and refrigerate for 2 hours to chill.
Whip 1 cup of heavy cream with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Sweet briar cream's emollient properties help to soften, moisturize, and soothe dry, chapped lips by filling in gaps between skin cells and locking in moisture.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sweet briar cream help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet briar's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and healthy-looking lip appearance.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosa rubiginosa, also known as sweet briar, creams helps with dry lips because of its rich antioxidant and moisturizing properties. The herb contains essential oils and vitamins that deeply nourish and hydrate dry, chapped lips, providing long-lasting relief. Rosa rubiginosa's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healthy lip tissue and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Regular use of Rosa rubiginosa creams can leave lips feeling soft, smooth, and revitalized.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Rosa rubiginosa have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in repairing and hydrating dry, chapped lips.
Phenolic acids: These compounds help maintain the skin's natural barrier function, locking in moisture and protecting against environmental stressors that can exacerbate dry lip conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain essential oils and antioxidants that help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Leaves: They are rich in tannins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to protect and heal dry lips.
Buds: They have a high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin on dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of rosa rubiginosa petals when they are fully open and in bloom.
Dry the harvested petals in a warm place for 3-5 days or using a dehydrator at 40°c.
Infuse 20g of dried rosa rubiginosa petals in 200ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with the infused oil in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dry, cracked lips, promoting healthy lip function.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, providing relief and soothing the skin.
Emollient properties: Thyme's emollients help to moisturize and soften dry lips, filling in cracks and crevices while protecting the skin from further drying and irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with dry lips because of its natural moisturizing and soothing properties. The herb contains thymol, a compound that has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to calm and protect dry, chapped lips. Additionally, thyme creams often contain other emollient ingredients like beeswax and coconut oil, which provide long-lasting hydration and help to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft and smooth. This makes thyme creams a popular natural remedy for dry lip relief.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: This phenolic compound helps soothe and hydrate dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting moisture retention.
Caryophyllene oxide: A terpene with anti-inflammatory properties, caryophyllene oxide helps calm irritation and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Thymol: An antimicrobial phenol, thymol helps combat infections that can contribute to dry lip conditions, while also reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, particularly thymol, which provides antibacterial and moisturizing properties.
Stems: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and protect dry lips.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Steep 10 grams of dried thymus vulgaris in 100 milliliters of jojoba oil at 60 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then mix in 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for 30 minutes before applying to the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's hydrating ingredients, such as glycerin and panthenol, work to lock in moisture and provide long-lasting relief from chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, allowing dry and damaged lips to recover quickly and naturally. This combination of benefits makes herbal sage creams an effective solution for dry and chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin on the lips, reducing redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy lip cell growth and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
Moisturizing properties: Sage contains natural oils and waxes that help to lock in moisture and soothe dryness on the lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, chapped lips.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing of dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn or evening when potency is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Blend dried salvia officinalis leaves with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:3:4.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the hot mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass containers for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with dry lips because of their soothing and moisturizing properties. The peppermint extract in these creams provides a cooling sensation that calms irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry lips. Additionally, the cream's emollient ingredients, such as coconut oil and shea butter, work to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and supple. This dual-action approach helps to not only relieve dryness but also promote healthy lip development.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing and soothing: Peppermint's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, calm irritation, and reduce dryness on the lips, providing relief from discomfort and chapping.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's menthol content has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a sense of comfort and ease.

Antioxidant and protectant: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and damage, while also promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol, which helps to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain high amounts of vitamins A and E, which work together to nourish and protect dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine them with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler set over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add 20ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated taking approximately 3 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 40°c before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Pour the cream into a sterilized container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, work to calm irritated skin and lock in moisture. This helps to reduce flakiness and discomfort associated with dry lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. Additionally, lemon balm's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to prevent infection and soothe redness, promoting healthy-looking lips.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and protecting them from further damage caused by environmental stressors.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: The moisturizing and emollient properties of lemon balm cream help to lock in moisture and provide long-lasting hydration to dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and supple.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause dry, chapped lips by preventing the growth of harmful microorganisms and promoting a healthy lip environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect dry lips.
Buds: The small, tightly closed buds on the lemon balm plant contain high concentrations of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help lock in moisture and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and chop them finely to release their essential oils.
Combine chopped melissa officinalis with sweet almond oil in a 10% herb-to-oil ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes under gentle stirring to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 20% beeswax-to-oil ratio by weight, whipping until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dry lips because they provide intense moisturizing properties that deeply hydrate the lip tissue. The natural oils and extracts present in ginger, such as glycerin and vitamin E, work together to lock in moisture and soothe dryness, leaving lips feeling soft, supple, and comfortable. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce irritation and redness, promoting healthy-looking lips that are less prone to flakiness and cracking.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress on the skin, which can cause lip dryness and chapping.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips.
Moisturizing properties: Herbal ginger creams often contain natural emollients like coconut oil, shea butter, or beeswax that help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhizomes help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Roots: Containing natural emollients and humectants, roots provide intense hydration and nourishment to dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes and peel off the skin to reveal the inner layer.
Grind 20 grams of peeled ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Combine 30 grams of beeswax pellets with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of the ginger powder to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined, taking 10 minutes to prepare.
Pour the mixture into small containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with dry lips because of their unique blend of natural ingredients, including the herb Echinacea purpurea. This herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, while its moisturizing properties help lock in moisture and hydrate the skin. Additionally, the cream's emollient-rich formula creates a barrier on the surface of the lips, shielding them from harsh environmental factors and promoting a smoother, softer appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds could help soothe and protect dry, irritated lips by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Hydrating and moisturizing effects: Some species of echinacea have been shown to possess humectant properties, which could contribute to the cream's ability to retain moisture and provide long-lasting hydration to dry lips.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Echinacea has been traditionally used to treat wounds and infections. In a lip care context, these properties might help accelerate the healing process of chapped or cracked lips by promoting tissue repair and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients, seeds provide moisturizing and nourishing properties to relieve dry lip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least three weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to soothe and moisturize the skin. The herb licorice root contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds that calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's rich emollient properties provide long-lasting hydration, filling in cracks and crevices to leave lips feeling soft, supple, and protected from further dryness. This natural remedy offers a gentle, non-irritating solution for parched, chapped lips, leaving them looking healthy and luscious.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid, help to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and healthy-looking lip appearance.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant compounds present in herbal licorice creams help to protect the lips from environmental stressors like UV rays, pollution, and cold weather, which can cause damage and dryness, thereby supporting overall lip health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, which provides moisturizing and soothing properties that help to alleviate dryness and irritation on the lips.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain compounds like liquiritin and isoliquiritin that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective for calming and protecting dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in sweet almond oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining 250ml of the infused oil.
Mix 10g of beeswax pellets and 20g of shea butter with the infused oil and heat mixture until melted and combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes then whip in 5 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers filling to the brim and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tamarind creams helps with dry lips because they provide long-lasting hydration and nourishment to the skin. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as glycerin and panthenol, help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. Additionally, tamarind's natural antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. As a result, tamarind creams offer effective relief from dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and comfortable for hours.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tamarind's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to lip cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy lip tissue.

Humectant properties: The mucilage present in tamarind helps retain moisture on the skin's surface, locking it in and providing long-lasting hydration to dry lips, while also soothing irritation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tamarind's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, chapped, or irritated lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, tamarind roots help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Barks: Tannins present in tamarind bark provide a natural astringent property, helping to reduce lip moisture loss and lock in hydration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried tamarindus indica seeds, 50ml of coconut oil, 20ml of sweet almond oil, and 10ml of beeswax.
Grind the dried tamarindus indica seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground tamarindus indica powder with the coconut oil, sweet almond oil, and beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c.
Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature, and whip the mixture with an electric mixer or spatula for 2-3 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shea butter tree creams helps with dry lips because of their rich moisturizing properties. The cream's key ingredient, shea butter, is derived from the nut of the African shea tree and is naturally packed with vitamins A and E, as well as fatty acids. These nutrients deeply nourish and hydrate dry lips, providing long-lasting relief and comfort. As a result, dry, chapped lips are soothed and rejuvenated, leaving them smooth and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing and hydrating: Shea butter tree creams help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, providing long-lasting hydration.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in these creams help to protect lips from damage caused by environmental stressors and inflammation, reducing dryness and irritation.

Protective and barrier-forming: The fatty acids and lipids in shea butter tree creams create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, preventing moisture loss and shielding lips from harsh conditions.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in vitamins A and E, which help to moisturize and nourish dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Contain healing compounds that soothe and calm dry, irritated skin, making them ideal for dry lips.
Buds: Similar to the sap of the tree, buds are rich in antioxidants that help to hydrate and protect dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 30g of dried and powdered vitellaria paradoxa bark and set aside for later use.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof container.
Add 30g of the powdered vitellaria paradoxa bark to the heat-proof container containing the beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden completely before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry lips are natural remedies that provide relief and hydration to chapped, cracked lips. They help by soothing, moisturizing, and protecting the skin. The best herbal creams for dry lips include Calendula officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, Aloe vera, Cocos nucifera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Symphytum officinale, Camellia sinensis, and Vitis vinifera. If left untreated, dry lips can lead to discomfort, pain, and difficulty speaking or eating, negatively impacting daily life and self-confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry lips that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and dermatitis. Herbal creams can provide relief from itchiness, inflammation, and redness associated with these conditions. Ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of dryness and discomfort. These herbal creams are gentle on the skin and can be used as an alternative to harsh chemical-based treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dry lips is a blend of Beeswax and Shea Butter-based cream infused with Aloe Vera and Tea Tree Oil. The Beeswax creates a protective barrier, while Shea Butter deeply moisturizes and nourishes the lips. Aloe Vera soothes and calms dryness, and Tea Tree Oil has antibacterial properties that prevent infections. This combination creates a hydrating and protective lip balm that helps to lock in moisture and speed up the healing process, leaving lips soft and smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with dry lips because of its remarkable soothing and moisturizing properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, reduce redness and irritation, providing quick relief from dry and chapped lips. The cream's emollient properties also help to lock in moisture, leaving lips soft, smooth, and hydrated. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections, promoting healthy lip tissue and preventing further dryness. This natural remedy effectively tackles dry lip issues.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These antioxidants help protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy lipid barrier and reducing dryness.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help soothe and hydrate dry lips, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in dry, cracked lips, promoting healing and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high content of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties beneficial for dry lip care.

Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making them useful in lip care products for soothing and protecting dry lips.
Roots: Although less commonly used, the roots of Calendula officinalis contain inulin, a prebiotic that can help soothe and moisturize dry lips, promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers in full bloom at dawn when the dew is still present on the petals.
Dry the flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of the herbal oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter to create the cream base.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with dry lips because they contain glycyrrhizic acid, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. This compound helps to soothe and calm dry, cracked lips by reducing inflammation and promoting hydration. The cream's emollient properties also help to lock in moisture, protecting the lips from environmental stressors and promoting healthy lip cell regeneration. As a result, glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide long-lasting relief and comfort for dry, chapped lips. Regular use can help to restore lip health and prevent future dryness.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry lips by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy barrier function.
Flavonoids: Contributes to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra, thereby protecting the skin and promoting the healing of dry, chapped lips.
Saponins: Exhibits moisturizing and emollient properties, helping to soften and hydrate dry lip skin, while also providing a protective layer against environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that provides anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to relieve dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for dry lips due to their moisturizing and hydrating properties, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin.

Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make creams for dry lips, as they contain a high concentration of saponins, which have emollient and protective properties that help to soften and protect dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder with 500ml of distilled water in a medium saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the liquid is reduced to 200ml.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until melted.
Combine the herbal liquid with the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined then pour into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with dry lips because they contain the active compound menthol, which provides a cooling and soothing sensation. The menthol in peppermint creams helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of menthol also help to calm and protect the lips from irritants and environmental stressors, promoting healthy lip tissue and a smooth, hydrated appearance. Regular application of peppermint creams can help to alleviate dry, chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with dry lips by providing a cooling sensation that temporarily numbs the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips.
Limonene: Contributes to the moisturizing and soothing properties of Mentha x piperita creams, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness and irritation of the lips.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect dry, irritated lips from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The menthol and menthone content in the leaves provide a cooling sensation and moisturizing properties to soothe dry lips.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain menthol and other compounds that help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness on the lips.
Roots: The roots of the plant are rich in antioxidants and other compounds that help to protect and nourish dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves from the garden during peak oil production time.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve properties.
Combine 1 cup of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180°f for 30 minutes to create a uniform consistency.
Blend 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil with the cooled mentha x piperita mixture to enhance moisturizing properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with dry lips because they contain soothing and moisturizing properties that help to hydrate and calm dry, chapped skin. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals such as calcium and potassium, which work together to lock in moisture and support the skin's natural barrier function. This helps to repair and protect dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and comfortable. Regular use can provide long-lasting relief from dry, irritated lips.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Helps to reduce inflammation and irritation on dry lips, promoting a soothing and calming effect.
Aloe-β-sitosterol: Exhibits moisturizing properties, helping to lock in moisture and protect dry lips from further dehydration.
Glucomannan: Acts as a natural humectant, drawing in moisture from the air and retaining it in the skin to alleviate dryness and flakiness on the lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of soothing and moisturizing compounds like aloe vera gel, which helps to hydrate and calm dry lips.
Seeds: They are used due to their oil-rich content, which is often extracted and incorporated into lip balms to provide nourishment and hydration to dry lips.
Leaves: They also contain aloin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of aloe barbadensis gel and mix it with 100g of distilled water in a bowl.
Add 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and add 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of candelilla wax.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens and emulsifies.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium. These nutrients help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness. The anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera also calm irritated skin, providing long-lasting relief and comfort to dry, chapped lips. Regular use of Aloe vera creams can help to heal and hydrate dry lips, leaving them soft and smooth.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: Help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from water loss, reducing dryness and cracking on the lips.
Vitamins (B, C, and E): Act as antioxidants to neutralize free radicals, promote collagen production, and improve skin elasticity, resulting in smoother and healthier-looking lips.
Mucopolysaccharides: Attract and retain moisture in the skin, creating a protective barrier that helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is rich in soothing and moisturizing properties.
Seeds: They are used because they contain aloe-emodin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Buds: They are used for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and protect dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of aloe vera gel from fresh aloe vera leaves and pure it in a blender for 30 seconds.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine 10g of shea butter and 10g of olive oil with the previous mixture and stir until smooth.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 10g of distilled water to the mixture and stir gently for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cocos nucifera creams helps with dry lips because of its natural moisturizing and soothing properties. The cream, derived from the coconut tree, contains fatty acids and vitamins that help lock in moisture and protect the skin from environmental stressors. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and irritation, promoting healthy lip tissue. As a result, Cocos nucifera cream provides long-lasting relief and hydration to dry, chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and comfortable.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Help to moisturize and lock in moisture, reducing dryness and flakiness of the lips.
Vitamin E: Antioxidant properties help to protect the lips from environmental stressors, promote collagen production, and enhance skin elasticity.
Phenolic acids: Anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry lips.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits (coconuts) are often used to make creams for dry lips due to their rich moisturizing properties and high content of fatty acids.
Seeds (Oil): Coconut seed oil is commonly used in lip balms and creams because it provides intense moisturization, helps to repair and protect dry lips.
Leaves (Oil): Coconut leaf oil is also used in some lip care products due to its antimicrobial and moisturizing properties, which help to soothe and heal dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of cocos nucifera meat from mature coconuts and dry it at 40°c for 24 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried coconut with 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes then let it cool down to 40°c.
Add 10 grams of beeswax to the cooled mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into containers and let it cool down to room temperature before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with dry lips because they contain the active compound tea tree oil, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing redness and irritation. The moisturizing properties of the cream also help to lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and comfortable. Additionally, the gentle and non-irritating nature of Melaleuca alternifolia creams makes them suitable for sensitive skin, providing relief from dry, cracked lips.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a natural moisturizer, helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Terpinen-4-ol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infection and promoting wound healing in dry, cracked lips.
Methyl eugenol: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and irritation associated with dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Buds: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, which help protect and nourish dry, chapped lips.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds that help to moisturize and protect dry lips from environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted source when they are in full bloom.
Steep the leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the melaleuca alternifolia tea and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to the saucepan mixture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with dry lips because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and emollient properties. The cream's active ingredient, derived from the plant's leaves, promotes the growth of new skin cells and reduces inflammation, helping to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips. The emollient properties of the cream also provide long-lasting hydration, locking in moisture and protecting the lips from environmental stressors, leaving them soft, smooth, and healthy-looking. Regular use can lead to improved lip health and a reduction in dryness.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked lips by promoting skin regeneration and hydration.
Saponins: Have emollient properties that help to soften and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect and repair dry, irritated lip tissue, promoting healthy skin and reducing lip dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used because they are rich in allantoin, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of allantoin and other beneficial compounds that help to heal and protect dry skin.
Stems: Stems are used because they contain a mix of allantoin and other compounds that help to hydrate and soften dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves of symphytum officinale in early morning when dew is still present.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper bags for 2-3 weeks in a warm dark place.
Make a decoction by boiling 50g of dried symphytum officinale leaves in 500ml of water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and mix it with 200g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled to a workable consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis creams helps with dry lips because they are rich in antioxidants and essential fatty acids. These nutrients moisturize and nourish the skin, providing long-lasting hydration. The anti-inflammatory properties of Camellia sinensis also soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and discomfort. As a result, the lips become softer, smoother, and more supple. Regular use of Camellia sinensis creams can help to lock in moisture and protect the lips from environmental stressors, leaving them looking and feeling healthier.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These flavonoid compounds help protect and moisturize dry lips by providing antioxidant properties that prevent further damage and promote skin health.
Theaflavins: Acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, theaflavins help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing irritation and discomfort.

Gallic acid: As an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, gallic acid helps reduce redness, inflammation, and bacterial infections associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting faster healing and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their moisturizing properties and ability to lock in moisture.
Seeds: Used for their high content of squalene, an emollient that helps to hydrate and protect dry lips.
Barks: Used for their astringent and soothing properties that help to calm and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Steep 5 teaspoons of camellia sinensis leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes to create a tea base.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove solids and excess tea particles.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the tea base with 1 ounce of beeswax and 1 ounce of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and cocoa butter melt.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera creams helps with dry lips because of their high antioxidant and moisturizing properties. The grape seed extract found in Vitis vinifera creams is rich in polyphenols, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. This cream also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and irritation, promoting healthy lip tissue. As a result, Vitis vinifera creams provide long-lasting relief and hydration to dry, cracked lips, leaving them smooth and soft. Regular use can help to prevent future dryness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and promote hydration, reducing dryness and irritation.
Phenolic acids: These acids have antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote collagen production, resulting in healthier and more moisturized lips.
Terpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Buds: They are rich in antioxidants and tannins, which help to soothe and protect dry lips.
Fruits: The juice and extract from Vitis vinifera fruits, also known as grapes, are used to create moisturizing creams due to their emollient and hydrating properties.
Seeds: The oil extracted from Vitis vinifera seeds is often used in creams for its moisturizing and emollient properties, helping to repair and protect dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in the early morning to ensure the highest concentration of essential oils.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for 7 to 10 days to prevent degradation.
Steep 50 grams of dried vitis vinifera in 1 liter of carrier oil at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove plant material and achieve clear consistency.
Add 5 percent of beeswax and 5 percent of vitamin e oil to the infused oil and stir until fully incorporated.
